title: Lemonade (LMND) Stock: Q1 Earnings Preview Ahead of April 29 Report
text: Analysts anticipate Q1 revenue of $254.03 million, representing a substantial 68% year-over-year increase compared to prior-year results. The company is expected to report a loss of $0.58 per share, which marks 32.6% year-over-year improvement despite continued unprofitability. The options market signals considerable volatility anticipation, with pricing suggesting approximately 14.66% potential price movement in either direction following the announcement. Currently trading at $65.95, the consensus Wall Street rating remains Hold, though Morgan Stanley recently upgraded the stock to Buy with an $85 price target. In-force premiums reached $1.24 billion at Q4, up 31% year-over-year. The company onboarded roughly 550,000 new policyholders in 2025, representing approximately 35% growth. Lemonade has consistently surpassed analyst expectations in recent quarters. A significant development involves Lemonade's specialized insurance offering for Tesla Full Self-Driving users, featuring approximately 50% reduced per-mile costs. Morgan Stanley's upgrade thesis emphasizes this first-mover advantage in autonomous vehicle coverage as potentially transformative. Analysts will monitor bad debt trends and interest expense impacts, alongside broader sector dynamics. Competitors showed modest outperformance recently, yet LMND has advanced 12.3% over the trailing month despite year-to-date declines of approximately 8%.
